result.stdout:
Usage:
  adocnet-docbook <input.adoc|directory> [options]

By default, output is written to a file with the same name and .xml
extension. Use -o - for stdout.

Options:
  -b, --backend <fmt>   Output format: html5, pdf, docbook5, epub (default: docbook)
  -o <file>             Write output to file (use '-' for stdout)
  -D, --destination-dir <dir>  Write output files to directory
  -a, --attribute <k=v> Set a document attribute
  -n, --section-numbers Auto-number section titles
  -e, --embedded        Wrap HTML in a full document with CSS theme
  --theme <name>        Select CSS theme: default, asciidoctor, clean
  --dump-ast            Print AST instead of rendering
  -w, --watch           Watch input file for changes and re-render
  -v, --verbose         Enable verbose output
  -q, --quiet           Suppress non-error output
  -r, --recursive       Process input directories recursively
  --config <file>       Load project configuration (default: discover adocnet.json)
  --extensions <path>   Load extensions from a DLL file (repeatable)
  --extension-dir <dir> Load all extension DLLs from directory (repeatable)
  --no-auto-extensions  Skip loading installed extensions from ~/.adocnet/extensions/
  -h, --help            Show help

Examples:
  adocnet-docbook README.adoc                       Convert to README.xml
  adocnet-docbook README.adoc -o -                  Convert to stdout
  adocnet-docbook README.adoc -o custom.xml        Convert to custom.xml
  adocnet-docbook docs/ -r -D build/                Convert directory to build/
  adocnet-docbook docs/ --watch -v                  Watch and rebuild on changes
  adocnet-docbook README.adoc -a version=2.0        Set document attribute
